items.title: Humanoid Robots Run Half Marathon in Beijing
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Humanoid robots take part in a race in Beijing. Billed as the world’s first humanoid marathon, the event is a testament to China’s ambition to lead the world in the development of advanced technologies from artificial intelligence to robots and chips.
--------

items.title: Will #AI replace CEOs in meetings?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>The CEO of a meeting-transcription company has developed a digital avatar that can fill in for him, and envisions a day when every leader has one. Bloomberg's Matt Boyle explains how Otter.ai could change boardroom forever.</article>

items.title: A sock-sorting vacuum shows home robots are in their early stages
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Roborock’s $1,900 vacuum cleaner is saddled with limitations, but it offers a glimpse at where home robots are headed. Dana Wollman reports.
--------

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>On this week’s Big Take podcast, @SheridanAsia tells @sarahsholde about her investigation into the rubber and palm oil company Socfin – and why a new EU law could bring reforms http://apple.co/3YEgDW7</article>

items.title: $64 Million Hong Kong Mansions Test Luxury Appetite
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Despite market volatility, a developer in Hong Kong is testing whether property is still an enticing investment for the ultra-rich. They're releasing a clutch of new mansions on the Peak, one of Hong Kong's swankiest addresses. Bloomberg's Venus Feng reports.
--------

items.title: Will GM's big bet on electric cars pay off? #tech #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>On this week's Big Take episode, David Gura and David Welch discuss GM's big bet on electric vehicles. Will the company weather the downturn and come out on top? And what would this ambition mean for electric vehicle owners and for the consumers who want them https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/big-take/id1578096201</article>

items.title: Inside the explosive feud that rocked Singapore’s richest family #singapore #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>For decades, Singapore’s Kwek family, owners of one of the city’s biggest developers, looked to be the model of a successful family-run business. But that image was shattered when the family patriarch filed a lawsuit against his son.

On today's Big Take Asia Podcast, host K. Oanh Ha sits down with Bloomberg’s Sheryl Lee and Dexter Low to examine what happened behind the boardroom doors of City Developments and how investors are navigating its fallout. 

Listen and subscribe here: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/big-take-asia/id1746141911

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Dan Ives, Global Head of Technology at Wedbush Securities, on the Nvidia chip restrictions. Technology stocks fell due to new US government restrictions on Nvidia chip exports to China and a disappointing report from ASML Holding NV, wiping out $155 billion in market value. He speaks with Bloomberg's Tom Keene and Paul Sweeney.

items.title: #Trump #tariffs are hurting one of Europe's largest logistical hubs #politics #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump’s erratic trade moves are hurting business at one of Europe’s largest logistics hubs.

At the port in Duisburg, Germany, shipping companies are taking departures out of their timetables, said Markus Bangen, who heads the company operating the world’s largest inland container harbor. They’re rattled by Trump’s tariffs, which have prompted manufacturers led by Volkswagen AG’s Audi to pause exports to the US.

“Within a few days, basic trust has been destroyed,” Bangen said in an interview. “Everyone who can stop deliveries to the US is doing so.”</article>

items.title: #China's #Xi Arrives in Cambodia for State Visit #politics #china
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Chinese President Xi Jinping promoted the idea of an “Asian family” and called for regional unity during a tour of Southeast Asia, in an apparent effort to counter US pressure on nations to limit trade ties with Beijing.

Xi landed in Phnom Penh on Thursday, kicking off the final leg of his three-nation tour as the Trump administration prepares to seek trade partners’ cooperation in encircling Beijing. The Chinese leader emphasized solidarity in a speech at a state dinner in Malaysia a day earlier, when the two nations signed wide-ranging deals in a sign of deepening economic ties.</article>

items.title: Chinese EV Maker XPeng Plans for Steady Global Expansion Despite Tariff War
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Electric vehicle manufacturer XPeng is accelerating its global expansion efforts despite geopolitical challenges, including the ongoing trade war between Washington and Beijing. XPeng CEO He Xiaopeng spoke exclusively with Bloomberg's Stephen Engle after the Chinese EV brand hosted a global launch for its flagship vehicle, the X9 minivan, in Hong Kong.
--------

items.title: First EU Leader Meets With Trump on Tariffs | Balance of Power: Late Edition
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Jared Bernstein, Former Chair of the White House Council of Economic Advisers, weighs in on President Donald Trump's continued criticism of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and whether or not the President has the ability to fire Powell. Sandi Bragar, Aspirtiant Chief Client Officer & Partner talks market volatility will continue as tariff uncertainty persist. Annaluisa Padilla, American Immigration Lawyers Association Former President states the Trump Administration is pushing the limit on what the Executive branch can do when discussing the Kilmar Abrego Garcia case.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Balance of Power Begins
00:02:35 - Bloomberg’s Wendy Benjaminson on Pres. Trump Hosting Italian PM Meloni, What to Make Of Trump Meloni Meeting for EU
00:04:52 - Bloomberg’s Michael Shepard on Pres. Trump Stating a TikTok Deal Will Be Good For China, Will There Be a Sale of TikTok
00:07:37 - Bloomberg’s Michael McKee on Pres. Trump Wanting to Force Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
00:10:33 - Jared Bernstein, Fmr. Chair of White House Council of Economic Advisers, on Trump’s Dislike For Jerome Powell, Fed Independence At Risk
00:17:59 - Sandi Bragar, Aspiriant Chief Client Officer & Partner, on S&P 500 Falling 1.5% This Week, Tariff Uncertainty Keeping Market Volatile
00:23:42 - Bloomberg’s David Voreacos, on SCOTUS to Hear Birthright Citizenship Case
00:26:35 - Annalusia Padilla, Fmr. Pres. of the American Immigration Lawyers Association, on Sen. Denied El Salvador Prison Visit, Trump Pushing Limit on Power
00:36:03 - Political Panel on President Trump’s Ongoing Criticism Of Fed Chair Powell, Could Trump Fire Powell, Risk of Fed Independence, Trump & Meloni Meeting
00:44:24 - Washington Lookahead: US & Iran Set for More Talks in Rome This Weekend Over Nuclear Programs
--------
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
